Question: Construct a table of values and plot the parametric equations: $$x ( t ) = t - 3 ,$$ $$y ( t ) = 2 t + 4 ; - 1 \le t \le 2.$$
Parameterize the curve given by $$x = y^{3} - 2 y .$$
Answer: $$\left .\begin{matrix}{a}x ( t ) = t^{3} - 2 t \\ y ( t ) = t\end{matrix}\right .$$

Question: Given the equations below, eliminate the parameter and write as a rectangular equation for $$y$$ as a function of $$x .$$ $$\left .\begin{matrix}{a}x ( t ) = 2 t^{2} + 6 \\ y ( t ) = 5 - t\end{matrix}\right .$$
Eliminate the parameter and write as a rectangular equation . $$\left .\begin{matrix}{a}x ( t ) = t^{2} \\ y ( t ) = \ln{} t t > 0\end{matrix}\right .$$
Eliminate the parameter from the given pair of parametric equations and write as a Cartesian equation: $$x ( t ) = 2 \cos{} t$$ and $$y ( t ) = 3 \sin{} t .$$
Write the given parametric equations as a Cartesian equation: $$x ( t ) = t^{3}$$ and $$y ( t ) = t^{6} .$$
